violations.text: Refrigerator door gaskets on cookline prep unit were observed in poor repair. PIC stated work order is in place. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2025
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023

Shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023

Bacon wrapped scallops, salmon, olive tapenade, and marinated shrimp were observed held at 46-47 degrees Fahrenheit in prep unit, cold drawers, and grill drawers. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per interview of PIC, foods were recently placed in unit. Additional ice was added to foods and foods were relocated to other refrigeration. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of PIC, Lobster Sherry Sauce and Chicken Broth were placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Foods date marked from previous day. Temperatures observed at 45-47 degrees Fahrenheit. Foods were voluntarily disposed by the PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023
Refrigerator door gaskets at the walk-in cooler for proteins and grill drawers were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/08/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the handsink by the dishmachine has an accumulation of black residue. Caulking was also observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/08/2023
Condensate line from upstairs equipment is draining into seafood prep sink. Condensate water must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 08/08/2023
Protective shielding in protein walk-in cooler was observed in poor repair. Physical facilities must be in good repair and proper adjustment. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/08/2023
